title: Fix: Remove stale port 8000/8001 fallbacks from dashboard and align ServerConfig default to 8002
analysis: Two port consistency issues: (1) dashboard config.py lists stale ports 8000/8001 as fallback URLs alongside the canonical 8002 — the dashboard will silently fall back to stale Claude Code MCP instances running old code. (2) fused-memory ServerConfig.port defaults to 8000 but config.yaml and convention use 8002. Fix is straightforward: remove stale ports from _DEFAULT_FUSED_MEMORY_URLS, change ServerConfig.port default from 8000 to 8002. Test impacts: dashboard/tests/test_scaffold.py asserts the 3-URL default list and count; dashboard/tests/test_memory.py has fallback/aggregation tests that relied on 3 default URLs (test_first_server_down_falls_through, test_successful_stats with 3xN aggregation, test_partial_failure_aggregates_available). These tests need to use explicit multi-URL configs to continue testing the fallback logic, which remains valid for env-var overrides. The mcp_tool_call unit tests use hardcoded URLs in mocks and are unaffected. fused-memory/tests/test_config_schema.py needs a new test for the port default.

steps.description: Add test_default_port_is_8002 to TestServerConfigTransport in fused-memory/tests/test_config_schema.py asserting ServerConfig().port == 8002. This test will FAIL because the current default is 8000.
Change ServerConfig.port default from 8000 to 8002 in fused-memory/src/fused_memory/config/schema.py line 73. Update the Field description to 'Server port (canonical: 8002)' for clarity.

Update TestConfigDefaults.test_config_defaults in dashboard/tests/test_scaffold.py to assert fused_memory_urls == ['http://localhost:8002'] (single canonical URL). Update TestConfigEnvOverrides.test_env_overrides to assert len(cfg.fused_memory_urls) == 1. These tests will FAIL because the defaults still include 8000/8001.
Remove 'http://localhost:8000' and 'http://localhost:8001' from _DEFAULT_FUSED_MEMORY_URLS in dashboard/src/dashboard/config.py, leaving only ['http://localhost:8002'].

Update dashboard/tests/test_memory.py tests that relied on 3-URL defaults: (a) TestGetMemoryStatus.test_first_server_down_falls_through — provide explicit multi-URL dashboard_config with 2 URLs (e.g. ports 9000, 9001) so one can fail and the other succeeds; update handler to fail on port 9000 instead of 8000. (b) TestGetQueueStats.test_successful_stats — change assertion from pending==9 (3x3) to pending==3 (1 server), since default config now has 1 URL. (c) TestGetQueueStats.test_partial_failure_aggregates_available — provide explicit multi-URL dashboard_config with 2 URLs (e.g. ports 9000, 9001), update handler to fail on port 9000 instead of 8000, assert pending==3 (1 successful x 3 pending).

design_decisions.decision: Keep fallback logic in get_memory_status and get_queue_stats even though defaults now have only one URL
design_decisions.rationale: Users can still configure multiple URLs via DASHBOARD_FUSED_MEMORY_URLS env var. The fallback iteration logic is correct and useful — the problem was only the defaults including stale ports.

design_decisions.decision: Update memory fallback/aggregation tests to use explicit multi-URL configs rather than deleting them
design_decisions.rationale: The fallback behavior is still valid functionality that should remain tested. Tests should construct explicit configs with synthetic ports (e.g. 9000, 9001) to exercise the multi-URL iteration path without depending on defaults.

design_decisions.decision: Leave mcp_tool_call unit tests using arbitrary port numbers (8000, 9998, 9999) unchanged
design_decisions.rationale: These tests mock the transport entirely and pass explicit URLs to the low-level function. The port numbers in these tests are arbitrary mock endpoints, not references to the canonical fused-memory port. Changing them would be churn with no value.

reuse.what: DashboardConfig dataclass constructor with explicit fused_memory_urls
reuse.how: Construct DashboardConfig(project_root=tmp_path, fused_memory_urls=['http://localhost:9000', 'http://localhost:9001']) to test multi-URL fallback without depending on defaults.
Reuse the same port-discriminating handler pattern but with different port numbers matching the explicit config URLs.
Add the new port default test as a method in the existing test class to maintain organization.
